army.title: Army
army.split.title: Split {name}
ability.flying.desc: Ignores obstacles and units; moves up to its speed.
ability.shooter.desc: Ranged attack with line of sight; melee penalty when out of ammo.
ability.doubleAttack.desc: Strikes twice in a single action.
ability.undead.desc: Morale locked at 0; immune to morale swings.
ability.mark.desc: Each hit applies a Mark charge (max 3, +8% damage taken per charge).
ability.consumeMarks.desc: Spends Marks for bonus damage, retaliation suppression, or immobilization.
ability.demonform.desc: Starts magic-resistant, transforms once per battle to hit harder.
ability.symbiosis.desc: Gains a growing bonus while it stays in place.
ability.shieldWall.desc: Defending grants a stronger defense bonus.
ability.unlimitedRetaliation.desc: Retaliates against every attack in the round.
ability.charge.desc: Damage bonus scaling with distance moved before the strike.
ability.lifeDrain.desc: Heals for part of the melee damage it deals.
ability.incorporeal.desc: Chance to fully dodge an incoming strike.
ability.strikeAndReturn.desc: Hits then returns to its position; the target does not retaliate.
ability.curseOnHit.desc: A hit may weaken the target (attack, defense, speed, or reduced damage).
ability.aura.desc: Alters enemy stacks' morale across the field.
ability.swarm.desc: Damage bonus when several allies surround the target.
ability.areaAttack.desc: Splashes enemies adjacent to the target.
ability.devourMarks.desc: Consumes all Marks on the field for damage and healing.
ability.breathAttack.desc: The strike also hits the enemy behind the target.
ability.taunt.desc: Adjacent melee attacks must target it.
ability.poisonSting.desc: A hit poisons the target: damage at the start of each round.
ability.firstStrike.desc: Acts before units of equal speed.
ability.spellcaster.desc: Casts a built-in spell a limited number of times per battle.
preBattle.title: Battle

combat.heroAttackPreview: ≈ {amount} damage to the chosen target (once per combat)
combat.retreatBody: The hero flees and ABANDONS the whole army, but survives. The battle is lost.
combat.surrenderBody: Pay {gold} gold to leave the battle KEEPING the surviving army. The battle is lost.
combatLog.immobilized: {unit} is immobilized and skips its turn

mapCard.effectLuck: +{amount} luck until the end of the next battle
mapCard.effectMorale: +{amount} morale until the end of the next battle
mapCard.effectLevelXp: Grants the XP missing for the next level

spell.invocation-elementaire.lore: The earth heaves and rises: stone elementals join the ranks, hewn for melee.

warMachine.catapulte.name: Catapult

town.tavernEmpty: No hero available for this faction.
town.guildEmpty: No mage guild built — build it to teach spells.

scenario.event-curee.name: The Great Cull
scenario.prologue.name: Prologue: Cendregarde
character.narrateur.name: Narrator
character.aldric.name: Aldric
character.seraphine.name: Seraphine
dlg.prologue.open.1: They say that in Cendregarde, even the bells pray. Since the First Eclipse the Griffin Empire has guarded this frontier — and the frontier, the elders say, guards something else.
dlg.prologue.open.2: The dead of the blessed cemetery have left their graves, Sister. Blessed graves. Explain that to me without blasphemy.
dlg.prologue.open.3: I cannot. That is exactly what worries me.
dlg.prologue.open.4: Raise the garrison. Relight the candles. And dig — not too deep.
dlg.prologue.ruines.1: The ruins sing false, Aldric. Raise the Fort first: what sleeps beneath does not love the light.
dlg.prologue.ruines.2: Then we shall make a great deal of light.
quest.prologue-relever.title: Raise Cendregarde
quest.prologue-garnison.title: The Watch of the March
daily.muster.title: Contract: Muster the Troop
daily.muster.desc: Enlist a company of footmen under your banner.
daily.warband.title: Contract: The Warband
daily.warband.desc: Reinforce your ranks with a handful of second-tier units.
daily.fortify.title: Contract: Fortify the Hold
daily.fortify.desc: Raise your town's Fort to hold the march.
daily.endure.title: Contract: Hold the Line
daily.endure.desc: Survive three days without losing your hero.
campaign.haven.name: Haven: the Frontier
campaign.haven.desc: Hold Cendregarde when the dead refuse their rest.
campaign.haven.ch1.title: The Signs
campaign.haven.ch2.title: Break the Siege
scenario.haven-ch2.name: Chapter 2: Break the Siege
campaign.haven.ch3.title: The Ashen Marches
scenario.haven-ch3.name: Chapter 3: The Ashen Marches
dlg.ch3.open.1: The siege is lifted, but the rift remains — gaping, out there in the Marches. While it bleeds, none of our dead may rest.
dlg.ch3.open.2: Then we shall close it with our own hands. Lead the column, Seraphine. This land will see dawn again.
quest.ch3-sceller.title: Seal the Rift
quest.ch3-sceller.desc: Defeat the necromancer holding the Ashen Marches and close the seal.
dlg.ch2.open.1: We held, knight. But Heresh does not retreat — it harvests. The seal beneath our feet calls its dead by our name.
dlg.ch2.open.2: Then we shall tell it so to its face. Gather the garrison — this time, we march.
quest.ch2-briser.title: Break the Siege
quest.ch2-briser.desc: Defeat the hero of Heresh pressing Cendregarde.
quest.aldric-serment.title: Aldric's Oath
quest.aldric-serment.desc: Lead the guard to the hero of Heresh — and decide their fate.
dlg.aldric.arc.1: These conscripts have followed me since the marches. I know every one of their names. I will not squander them.
dlg.aldric.arc.2: The bows are drawn, knight. What remains is what you will do with Heresh once it kneels.
dlg.aldric.arc.3: The hero of Heresh is beaten, disarmed. He awaits my verdict. What becomes of an enemy who raised our dead against us?
dlg.aldric.arc.merciful: Spare him — one more corpse redeems none of ours.
dlg.aldric.arc.ruthless: Execute him — let his end seal the rift he reopened.
quest.seraphine-visions.title: Séraphine's Visions
quest.seraphine-visions.desc: Séraphine hears the seal singing. Discern where her visions come from — and what to do with them.
dlg.seraphine.arc.1: Ever since we neared the rift, I dream with open eyes. Pillars of light — or is it the seal that speaks to me? I no longer know who prays within me.
dlg.seraphine.arc.2: Your visions have led us to every victory, Sister. But a voice rising from a seal we are about to reopen… beware what it promises.
dlg.seraphine.arc.3: The rift lies before us, and the voice grows clear. Elrath enlightens me — or what sleeps below is calling. Should I heed it, or fall silent?
dlg.seraphine.arc.faith: Heed it — if this is Elrath's light, to deny it would be the true sin.
dlg.seraphine.arc.doubt: Fall silent — I will seal my visions with the rift, and trust only steel.
campaign.necropolis.name: Necropolis: the Harvest
campaign.necropolis.desc: Return to Asha the souls Cendregarde holds captive.
campaign.necropolis.ch1.title: The Call
campaign.necropolis.ch2.title: The Harvest
scenario.necropolis-ch1.name: Chapter 1: The Call
scenario.necropolis-ch2.name: Chapter 2: The Harvest
character.vhalen.name: Vhalen
character.mere-corbeau.name: Mother Raven
dlg.necro.ch1.open.1: Beneath Cendregarde, the dead cannot find the Wheel. To Heresh this is no horror but a summons: something holds the souls back — they must be freed.
dlg.necro.ch1.open.2: The seal starves them, Mother. Each trapped soul is a page torn from Asha's ledger. We come to sew it back.
dlg.necro.ch1.open.3: Gently, archivist. One does not harvest in anger. We gather what is ripe, and tuck in the rest.
dlg.necro.ch1.fort.1: Raise these walls first. A sanctuary of bone, that none may trouble those we call back.
dlg.necro.ch2.open.1: Haven calls it desecration. They weep over empty graves and never see the souls drowning within.
dlg.necro.ch2.open.2: Then we shall show them. To reap the living who stand in the way is not cruelty: it is arithmetic. The Wheel is waiting.
quest.necro-ch1-sanctuaire.title: The Sanctuary of Bone
quest.necro-ch1-sanctuaire.desc: Raise the Fort of Cendregarde to shelter the harvest.
quest.necro-ch2-moisson.title: The Refused Harvest
quest.necro-ch2-moisson.desc: Defeat the hero of Haven who forbids the reaping.
quest.vhalen-sceau.title: Vhalen's Archive
quest.vhalen-sceau.desc: Vhalen has read what none dare read: the seal has fed Heresh souls since time began. To mend it would starve his own people.
dlg.vhalen.arc.1: I cross-checked the registers back to the first dynasties. The seal does not hold our dead — it diverts them to us. Heresh has fed on the rift for a thousand years.
dlg.vhalen.arc.2: Then to close it would return those souls to the Wheel… and leave our dwellings empty. You carry knowledge that weighs a kingdom, archivist.
dlg.vhalen.arc.3: Doctrine says: return every soul to Asha. Survival says: keep the rift open. I hold both in the same hand. Which do I close?
dlg.vhalen.arc.repair: Mend the seal — a faith that cheats on souls is no faith at all.
dlg.vhalen.arc.feed: Leave the rift — a dead people returns no soul to anyone.
quest.corbeau-enfant.title: The Child of Ashguard
quest.corbeau-enfant.desc: A small soul is caught in the seal, neither dead nor returned. Mother Crow hears it weeping; freeing it may demand a bargain with the Haven.
dlg.corbeau.arc.1: Hush. Do you hear it? Under the seal, a child of Ashguard waits to be rocked. The seal holds her halfway — neither living, nor returned to the Wheel. No one has gathered her in.
dlg.corbeau.arc.2: The register does not know this soul. She is off the ledger, off the doctrine. To touch her is to prise the seal open by a finger's width — and that finger, the Haven holds from the other side.
dlg.corbeau.arc.3: I can reach out to the Haven's priest: together we loosen the seal and I rock the child one last time. Or I let her sleep, because one does not bargain with the light. What does a midwife of souls do?
dlg.corbeau.arc.pact: Bargain with the Haven — no child's soul deserves to wait a thousand years.
dlg.corbeau.arc.refuse: Let the child sleep — a bargain with the light is always paid twice.
campaign.arcane.name: The Hunters: the Lock
campaign.arcane.desc: Hold the rift beneath the graveyards — for none else knows the key.
campaign.arcane.ch1.title: The Outpost
campaign.arcane.ch2.title: The Lock
scenario.arcane-ch1.name: Chapter 1: The Outpost
scenario.arcane-ch2.name: Chapter 2: The Lock
character.evadne.name: Evadne Corvel
character.marchmont.name: Professor Marchmont
dlg.arcane.ch1.open.1: The Circle has read the registers, Huntmaster. Beneath these marshes an ancient seal is failing — and what it held is waking.
dlg.arcane.ch1.open.2: Then we plant the outpost here, on the lock. The other houses pray; we hold the door.
dlg.arcane.ch1.fort.1: A fort first. Walls, candles, a Mark at every corner. What comes will not cross this line.
dlg.arcane.ch2.rencontre.1: You again. The Church tolerates your hunts, Lady Corvel — not your digging beneath our graveyards.
dlg.arcane.ch2.rencontre.2: Your graveyards sit on our lock, knight. I have the key graven on half my face. You have candles.
dlg.arcane.ch2.rencontre.3: The candles have kept the night out for a thousand years.
dlg.arcane.ch2.rencontre.4: No. We keep it out. The candles are so you can sleep. Keep your walls, pray hard. And if one of my Marks lights on one of your dead… do not mourn him twice.
dlg.arcane.ch2.pacte: Let the Hunters through — the lock is worth more than pride.
dlg.arcane.ch2.refus: Bar them from the crypt — our dead are not your workshop.
quest.arcane-ch1-avant-poste.title: The Outpost
quest.arcane-ch1-avant-poste.desc: Build the Fort on the lock to anchor the Hunt.
quest.arcane-ch2-la-serrure.title: Hold the Lock
quest.arcane-ch2-la-serrure.desc: Defeat the hero of Haven guarding the crypt above the rift.
quest.evadne-verrou.title: Evadne's Lock
quest.evadne-verrou.desc: The rune grafted onto Evadne's face answers the Cendregarde rift. The seal recognizes her — the question is as what.
dlg.evadne.arc.1: Half my face burns as we near the crypt. It is no war-scar: it is a shard of the lock. The seal and I share the same engraving.
dlg.evadne.arc.2: Then the rift is calling you, Lady Corvel. A key always ends up wanting its lock. Mind who turns whom.
dlg.evadne.arc.3: The seal knows me. I can open what none can open — or tear out the rune and be only a huntress again. Am I the key, or the one who carries it?
dlg.evadne.arc.embrace: Own it — a key that refuses its lock serves no one.
dlg.evadne.arc.sever: Tear out the rune — I hunt the seal, I do not obey it.
quest.marchmont-mensonge.title: The Dean's Lie
quest.marchmont-mensonge.desc: My owl brought back founding records no one was meant to reopen. The Academy was not built to seal the rift — but to hide it. The question is who should learn of it.
dlg.marchmont.arc.1: My owl combed the vaults beneath the library. These pages date to the founding. 'Know, mark, cut down' — the first oath did not speak of cutting down. It spoke of keeping silent. We guard a lie, not a rift.
dlg.marchmont.arc.2: You hold enough to undo the whole order, Dean. An oath revealed false, and every Mark we lay becomes suspect. Is this a truth, or a weapon?
dlg.marchmont.arc.3: I can read these pages before the assembled Circle — and watch the Academy doubt itself for the first time. Or return them to the vault, and let doctrine hold what truth could not. Does a Dean of Seals also seal lies?
dlg.marchmont.arc.reveal: Reveal the pages — an order that fears its founding does not deserve to keep it.
dlg.marchmont.arc.protect: Bury the pages — a lie that holds the rift is worth more than a truth that opens it.
bark.arcane.necropolis.1: Your Marks do not bite the dead, hunters. They bleed no longer.
bark.arcane.necropolis.2: Come closer. The Wheel is hungry, and you are still warm.
bark.arcane.necropolis.3: A lock? We buried your keys with your fathers.
campaign.vox.name: Vox Arcana: the Honmoon
campaign.vox.desc: The Academy guards the barrier; when the dead crowd against it, the stage becomes a rampart.
campaign.vox.ch1.title: The breach
scenario.vox-ch1.name: Chapter 1: The breach
character.hermione.name: Hermione
character.rumi.name: Rumi
dlg.vox.ch1.open.1: The Honmoon trembles, Rumi. Beneath the Academy the barrier is fraying — and old dead things are climbing through the tear.
dlg.vox.ch1.open.2: Then we sing loud and strike true. The other houses chant; we hold the stage until the light returns.
dlg.vox.ch1.open.3: Walls first. The rest will follow the beat.
dlg.vox.ch1.fort.1: A fort to anchor the Honmoon. Ramparts, spotlights, one held note every turn. Nothing crosses our chorus.
quest.vox-ch1-honmoon.title: Hold the Honmoon
quest.vox-ch1-honmoon.desc: Build the Fort to anchor the barrier and push back the breach.
bark.vox.necropolis.1: Your stage is a graveyard that forgot itself, singers.
bark.vox.necropolis.2: Sing, then. The dead only applaud silence.
bark.vox.necropolis.3: The Honmoon? A curtain. We know what waits behind it.
campaign.dungeon.name: The Dungeon: the Descent
campaign.dungeon.desc: Banished underground, the dark elves rise again — not to see the sky, but to take back what was taken from them.
campaign.dungeon.ch1.title: The Descent
scenario.dungeon-ch1.name: Chapter 1: The Descent
character.raelag.name: Raelag
character.shadya.name: Shadya
dlg.dungeon.ch1.open.1: They drove us beneath the stone, Shadya, thinking they buried us. All they did was teach the shadows to walk.
dlg.dungeon.ch1.open.2: The Sylvans still hold the heights. Their unicorns shine like wishes. Let me show them what wishes are worth.
dlg.dungeon.ch1.open.3: Walls first. Let the Dungeon take root before the forest wakes.
dlg.dungeon.ch1.fort.1: A fort cut from black rock. Let them come: beneath our walls, their light is just one more target.
quest.dungeon-ch1-descent.title: Take root in the dark
quest.dungeon-ch1-descent.desc: Build the Fort to anchor the Dungeon's advance before the Sylvan assault.
bark.dungeon.sylvan.1: Back to your trees, cousins. The deep does not forgive the light.
bark.dungeon.sylvan.2: Your unicorns bleed like everything else. Come closer.
bark.dungeon.sylvan.3: You disowned us. Tonight we disown you in turn — for good.
campaign.dungeon.ch2.title: The Shadow Brood
scenario.dungeon-ch2.name: Chapter 2: The Shadow Brood
dlg.dungeon.ch2.open.1: The dead of the Necropolis prowl our borders, Raelag. They scent our broods the way one scents easy prey.
dlg.dungeon.ch2.open.2: Let them come count our hydras. You cannot kill what has already learned to die a thousand times.
dlg.dungeon.ch2.open.3: Then feed the brood. A Warlock Guild, and the dark itself will take up arms.
dlg.dungeon.ch2.guild.1: The Guild awakens. Every spell I seal within it is one more head on our hydra.
quest.dungeon-ch2-brood.title: Feed the brood
quest.dungeon-ch2-brood.desc: Build the Warlock Guild to arm the dark against the undead.
bark.dungeon.necropolis.1: Your bones are old, lich. Ours, we grow fresh.
bark.dungeon.necropolis.2: Death obeys you? Shadow obeys us. And shadow has no end.
bark.dungeon.necropolis.3: We respect your art. Enough to teach it back to you, inside out.
campaign.dungeon.ch3.title: The Lock
scenario.dungeon-ch3.name: Chapter 3: The Lock
dlg.dungeon.ch3.open.1: Ashguard. The knights of Haven keep it like a treasure. They never understood it was a lock.
dlg.dungeon.ch3.open.2: And a lock, Raelag, calls for a key. We do not come to break it. We come to own it.
dlg.dungeon.ch3.open.3: Then sink its well beneath their walls. Let the dark rise to the light — and put it out.
dlg.dungeon.ch3.well.1: The Cursed Well plunges beneath the seal. What Ashguard held back, we will turn against them.
quest.dungeon-ch3-seal.title: Turn the lock
quest.dungeon-ch3-seal.desc: Build the Cursed Well to reach the seal of Ashguard before Haven does.
bark.dungeon.haven.1: Pray away, knights. Your gods live above. We come from below.
bark.dungeon.haven.2: Your light makes such lovely targets in the dark.
bark.dungeon.haven.3: The seal is not yours. It only ever belonged to those who dare descend.
bark.haven.heresh.1: Your candles gutter, knight. The night does not tire.
bark.haven.heresh.2: Give back those souls, or we take them one by one.
bark.haven.heresh.3: Cendregarde will fall as all walls fall: slowly, then all at once.
handoff.title: Turn change
handoff.pass: Pass the device to the next player.
building.townHall.lore: Here they tally gold and the living. Both ledgers thin in the Marches, yet the clerk still dips his quill each morning.
building.fort.lore: A wall never asks who knocks. In the Marches that is a virtue: by night, few knock to be welcomed.
building.mageGuild.lore: Seven spells on the wall, an eighth left untaught. Apprentices sleep with the door shut, seal-side.
building.market.lore: Salt for copper, rumor for silence — all of it changes hands. Everything has a price; fear rises at dusk.
building.tavern.lore: The wine is sour and the stories better. Here you hire strong arms and bury names you would rather forget.
building.forge.lore: The smith nails together ballistas before horseshoes: in the Marches, no one rides out for pleasure.
spell.trait-de-feu.lore: The first flame you learn, the last you forget. One pointed finger, and the dark steps back a pace.
spell.boule-de-feu.lore: It knows neither friend nor rank. Careful mages cast it far; the rest are buried close.
spell.soin.lore: Water remembers whole flesh and reminds it. A brief balm — but the night runs long.
spell.benediction.lore: A word of light laid upon the shoulder. In the Marches, you take Elrath where he still flickers.
spell.bouclier-de-pierre.lore: Earth lends its patience to the bleeding. It promises nothing; it holds longer than fear.
spell.affaiblissement.lore: You don't break the foe — you steal his footing. Steel does the rest on its own.
spell.hate.lore: Wind slips into the legs and drives out hesitation. Well cast, it decides who strikes first.
spell.lenteur.lore: You thicken the air around a foe until he wades through honey. Patience is a weapon.
spell.eclair-magique.lore: No school, no color — only a will that bites. The pauper's spell, and it kills just as well.
spell.dissipation.lore: Unmaking sometimes beats making. You breathe out another's enchantments like a candle.
spell.purification.lore: Clear water carries off poisons, curses and shackles; only the stack's friendly strength remains.
spell.armure-fondue.lore: Metal recalls it was once liquid. Under the heat, the finest cuirass runs to the ground.
spell.brasier.lore: A sheet of fire that lingers on the fallen. In the Marches, the dead are burned as a precaution.
spell.jet-de-glace.lore: Water drawn to a point, and the cold that bites after the blade. Simple, clean, honest.
spell.brouillard-glacial.lore: A mist that freezes joints and clouds sight. The foe gropes forward; your ranks advance.
spell.grande-guerison.lore: The full tide of the water art. It stitches the living — it will not call back those who have gone.
spell.seisme.lore: The earth opens just enough to remind all it is waiting. Beneath the Marches, it waits for much.
spell.muraille-de-pierre.lore: You raise a slab of rock where a rampart was missing. It will fall; until then, hold the line.
spell.choc-electrique.lore: The sky lends a bolt without a cloud. Brief, white, and the smell of storm stays on the tongue.
spell.tornade.lore: Wind knotted into a column tears ranks apart and flings them. You do not argue with the tornado.
spell.chaine-d-eclairs.lore: Lightning hates being alone: it leaps from body to body, each jump a little paler than the last.
spell.aura-protectrice.lore: A quiet ring of favor about your own: a little poise, a little guard. Small, but you feel it.
spell.marque-du-guetteur.lore: A sign etched on the quarry, unseen only by itself. What is marked does not escape twice.
spell.entraves-runiques.lore: Runes close on the ankles like a cold snare. The foe stays; the hunt catches up to him.
spell.volee-de-dagues.lore: Every mark becomes a target. The blades find each brand and tear it open.
spell.silence-scelle.lore: A seal upon the throat. The words die before they are spoken, and the spell with them.
spell.bannissement.lore: What was summoned can be unmade. The rite sends the conjured thing back where it belongs — nowhere.
spell.heure-de-la-curee.lore: When the horn sounds, the marked have no guard left. The pack strikes, and nothing strikes back.
spell.mue-ephemere.lore: A breath of mist, and the hunter is gone — until she chooses to be seen again.
spell.pas-de-brume.lore: A step into the mist here, a step out of it elsewhere. Between the two there is nothing — not even the distance.
spell.ville-portail.lore: A fold of air carries the hero home within his walls. In the Marches, coming back alive is worth sixteen mana.
spell.barriere-du-honmoon.lore: The song arches into a vault above the faithful. While the voice holds, blows slide off the Honmoon.
spell.chant-de-courage.lore: A refrain the whole company takes up: fear lowers its head, and the blades rise again.
spell.dissonance.lore: A false note struck on purpose. The enemy hunts for the beat and never finds it; his guard comes undone.
spell.rappel.lore: The song's final bridge calls the fallen back to the stage. Some return for one last verse.
artifact.lame-aiguisee.lore: Honed too often, it is more edge than blade. It never learned to stop in time.
artifact.egide-de-pierre.lore: Heavy as regret, sure as a wall. It won't make you swift — only still standing.
artifact.orbe-de-savoir.lore: In it you read what you fear to learn. The wise keep it under a cloth, seal-side.
artifact.trefle-chance.lore: Plucked from a grave, they say. Luck in the Marches rarely comes but that another has lost it.
artifact.grimoire-arcanique.lore: Bound in leather and impatience. While worn, one spell too many comes to your fingers — Fireball, always Fireball.
artifact.bottes-de-sept-lieues.lore: Each stride is worth seven. Pull them on at dawn and the horizon, suddenly, draws near.
artifact.longue-vue.lore: A brass tube and two patient lenses. It brings nothing closer — it only teaches you what was already watching for you.
artifact.pendentif-de-bravoure.lore: While it hangs on the leader's chest no company wavers: fear loses its grip on all who follow.
artifact.cape-du-refus.lore: Woven of threads that drink spells. Incantations slide off the army like rain off oil.
artifact.talisman-de-constance.lore: Graven with an unbending oath. Neither curse nor weakening hex finds purchase on those who bear it.
resourceDetail.title: Resources
